Occulted flare from yet unnumbered Active Region rotating onto the Earth-facing disk
Occulted flare from yet unnumbered Active Region presently rotating to the Earth-facing disk; immediately follows the preceding M-class flare from the same region.
Occulted flare from the yet unnumbered Active Region rotating onto the Earth-facing solar disk.
Occulted flare from the unnumbered Active Region soon rotating onto the Earth-facing disc.
This is a double peaked flare which originates from prolonged brightening from AR 3182.
Simultaneous flaring from AR3194 near S24W50 was also observed.
Some simultaneous flaring was also observed by AR3192 in the northern hemisphere at the time of the flare associated with AR3190 best seen in SDO AIA 131.
Both Active Regions 3190 (southern hemisphere) and 3192 (northern hemisphere) flared simultaneously and contributed to the observed M1.3-class flare.

This flare occurred beyond the NW limb of the Earth-facing disk where two to three Active Regions are clustered together. It is not fully clear which Active Region caused the flare, but based on magnetic configuration and visibility of the flare itself, it likely originated from AR13192.

The flare was analyzed in GOES-16 131 imagery due to an SDO outage at the time.
Active Region 13213 has rotated off the Earth-facing disk, but this flare most likely originated from this region beyond the limb.
